prose: **Ethylene Frontier-Orbital Gap** — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, PySCF, exact integrals), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: provided input `ethylene.xyz`, used as-is (single-point, not re-optimized at DFT). SCF converged in 6 cycles; no warnings. Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = **7.4357 eV** (Koopmans-style orbital energy difference: HOMO at −7.6156 eV, LUMO at −0.1799 eV). Additional frontier orbitals reported: HOMO−2 (−11.488 eV), HOMO−1 (−9.884 eV), LUMO+1 (1.720 eV), LUMO+2 (2.380 eV). Koopmans-derived vertical ionization potential (7.6156 eV) and electron affinity (0.1799 eV) are also returned. The gap represents the orbital-based estimate of ionization/affinity energy; it is not an excitation energy without further correction. Total electronic energy: −78.6235 Hartree.
